nodes.context: Hispanic American street gang in San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles.
Street gang; former members founded Vineland Boys.
Prison gang; controls Sureño coalition; clashed with Vineland Boys.
Coalition of gangs under Mexican Mafia; Vineland Boys joined.
Criminal organization; allied with Vineland Boys.
Israeli mafia group; allied with Vineland Boys for ecstasy trafficking.
Mexican drug cartel; Vineland Boys had ties to it.

edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland Boys gang was founded in [Sun Valley, Los Angeles] in January 1988 by former members and associates of the 18th Street gang.
edges.context: Vineland Boys was founded by former members of 18th Street gang.

edges.evidence_quote: the Vineland Boys also clashed with the Mexican Mafia until the gang's founder Teddy Lopez y Alberto Ruiz was murdered by Mexican Mafia members at a Monterey Park nightclub in 1998.
edges.context: Vineland Boys clashed with Mexican Mafia until 1998.

edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland boys have since joined the Mexican Mafia-controlled Sureño gang coalition.
edges.context: Vineland Boys joined the Sureño coalition.

edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland Boys have formed alliances with Armenian Power and the Abergil crime family.
edges.context: Vineland Boys formed an alliance with Armenian Power.

edges.evidence_quote: The Vineland Boys have formed alliances with Armenian Power and the Abergil crime family. The gang allegedly first teamed up with Abergils in 2000, offering protection and cooperation to the Israeli mafia's ecstasy trafficking ring operating in their territory.
edges.context: Vineland Boys allied with Abergil crime family for protection and cooperation in ecstasy trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: revealing the Vineland Boys' ties to the Sinaloa Cartel
edges.context: Vineland Boys had ties to Sinaloa Cartel.
